verification_html: Twenty API key (Bearer), self-serve, REST+GraphQL auto-generated Confirmed: API key via Settings > API & Webhooks, Bearer auth header, REST + GraphQL both auto-generated from workspace schema, multiple community MCP servers exist. HIT DealCloud OAuth2, gated behind admin approval, REST API Confirmed OAuth2 Client Credentials flow, tokens expire in 900s. Confirmed gated: API access must be explicitly enabled by a site admin under User Management. Bonus finding not in first pass: DealCloud is launching an MCP server in client preview as of July 2026. HIT + Pylon OAuth2, self-serve, REST + MCP More nuanced than the first pass: the MCP server uses OAuth and needs a Member/Admin seat, but the separate REST API token can only be created by an Admin - two different access rules for two different surfaces on the same product. PARTIAL GoHighLevel OAuth2, self-serve dev account, REST API Confirmed: OAuth2 Authorization Code flow for public/marketplace apps, or a Private Integration Token for internal-only use - both self-serve from a free developer account. HIT Fathom No public API found, gated/limited Wrong. Fathom shipped a public API (Bearer token, user-scoped, 60 req/min) available to all users, plus multiple community MCP servers. This was a first-pass guess based on Fathom's older reputation as 'no API', not current fact. MISS GitHub OAuth2/PAT/GitHub App, self-serve, official MCP server No live check needed - GitHub's REST/GraphQL API and official MCP server are extremely well documented and stable; used as a control/sanity check rather than a real verification target. HIT (control) Consensus OAuth requested, gated, limited public API Split finding the first pass missed entirely: the MCP server (mcp.consensus.app) is instant self-serve with automatic OAuth and no account needed for basic queries. It's the separate full REST API that's gated ('available by application only', $0.10/call). First pass called the whole product 'gated' when the agent-relevant path (MCP) is actually open. MISS Waterfall.io API key, gated, needs a sales conversation Wrong. Waterfall.io hands out an API key immediately on signup, with no long-term contract or upfront payment required, per their own docs. First pass conflated 'enterprise-sounding B2B data vendor' with 'sales-gated', which turned out false here. MISS
